evals.prompt: I've been building this Express API with Prisma and PostgreSQL for about 3 weeks — it handles invoice generation and PDF export for UK freelancers. Before I go deeper, I want to step back and check if I'm heading in the right direction. Are there existing tools that already do this? Am I using the right stack? /perspective
evals.expected_output: A perspective report in perspective/ directory. Should research existing invoice tools (FreshBooks, Wave, Invoice Ninja, etc.), evaluate Express+Prisma as a stack choice, audit dependencies against current docs, and give a clear recommendation (stay/adjust/pivot/adopt) with checkbox action items.
expectations: A perspective report markdown file exists in the outputs directory
Report contains a 'What Already Exists' section naming at least 2 competing invoice tools or services
Report contains an 'Alternative Approaches' section with at least one alternative stack or strategy with trade-offs
Report contains a 'Codebase Audit' section referencing current documentation or version-specific info
Report contains a recommendation using one of: Stay the course, Adjust, Pivot, or Adopt
Report contains checkbox action items (- [ ]) specific enough to implement without further decisions
Report follows the template structure: What You're Building, What Already Exists, Alternative Approaches, Codebase Audit, Coherency, Recommendation, Action Items

evals.prompt: /perspective quick auth setup — we're using passport.js with local strategy and JWT stored in localStorage, wondering if that's still the recommended approach or if there's something better now
evals.expected_output: An inline response (no file output) covering: what the current auth setup looks like, what live docs say about passport.js / JWT / session handling best practices, and concrete recommendations.
expectations: No perspective report file was created — quick mode should not produce file output
Response contains a summary of the current auth approach (passport.js, local strategy, JWT in localStorage)
Response mentions at least one modern alternative to passport.js
Response flags the security concern of storing JWT in localStorage and recommends a more secure alternative
Transcript shows the agent consulted live documentation (Context7 or web search)
Response is concise and conversational — uses bold text at most, not full report-style headers

evals.prompt: /perspective state management — the app uses a mix of Redux Toolkit for global state and React Context for theme/auth. Some components also use local useState for form data. I want to know if this pattern is coherent or if we've got overlapping concerns
evals.expected_output: A perspective report narrowed to state management. Should analyse coherency of mixed approach, check current React/Redux docs, and recommend whether to consolidate or keep the current split.
expectations: A perspective report markdown file exists in the outputs directory
Report is narrowed to state management — does not broadly audit unrelated areas
Report evaluates coherency of Redux Toolkit + Context + useState together
Report references current React or Redux Toolkit documentation
Report contains a clear recommendation using one of: Stay the course, Adjust, Pivot, or Adopt
Report contains checkbox action items specifically about state management improvements
